items.title: Trump also announced US oil companies will rebuild Venezuelan oil industry | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump said the U.S. will oversee Venezuela until a "safe, proper, and judicious" transition can be arranged. This announcement follows an overnight military operation in which Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro and his wife were seized in the capital city of Caracas.
During a press conference after the raid, Trump promised to rebuild the South American nation's infrastructure and oil industry while maintaining a U.S. military presence for as long as necessary. Trump also dismissed the idea of working with Venezuela's exiled opposition leader, María Corina Machado.

Chapters:
0:00 Trump says US to run Venezuela after Maduro raid
0:58 Donald Trump | US President
3:15 Benjamin Alvarez Gruber, DW Correspondent 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Trump press conference on US attack on Venezuela | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The US has confirmed that its military has carried out a large-scale operation in Venezuela. President Donald Trump said Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro was "captured and flown out of the country" by US troops and law enforcement authorities. Footage on social media captured a series of blasts in the capital Caracas while jets were seen flying over the city at around 2 am local time. Venezuela has declared a national emergency and said it will "resist the presence of foreign troops."

items.title: Why Iran has condemned US military action in Venezuela | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>As one of its close allies, Iran ‍condemned the ‌attacks on ‌Venezuela as "a blatant violation of ‍its national ​sovereignty ​and territorial integrity." All of this comes as tensions between Washington and Tehran are on the rise, as well.
That's because the protests all over Iran show no sign of slowing down: People across the country are continuing to voice their anger at the Islamic Republic. According to official sources at least ten people have now been killed, most of them protesters. And just on Friday the U-S president warned that if Tehran "violently kills peaceful protesters," the United States "will come to their rescue."

00:00 Iran condemns US military action in Venezuela
01:26 DW speaks with Sima Sabet, Iran expert and a former talk show host at Iran International

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Venezuela condemns US after air strikes hit Caracas | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Venezuela has declared a national emergency and blamed the US for explosions that shook its capital Caracas. Footage on social media captured a series of blasts with rising plumes of smoke, while low-flying jets were seen flying over the city. Witnesses say there were at least seven explosions at around 2 am local time, resulting in a power outage in the southern part of Caracas. The CBS and Reuters news outlets report that the Pentagon is behind the strikes, which were ordered by President Donald Trump. This comes after weeks of often deadly strikes in the region, which Washington says have targeted drug-smuggling boats and a Venezuelan port, used for drug smuggling. DW's Oskar Schlenker provides analysis of the situation.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Why Zelenskyy chose popular spy chief and potential political rival Budanov for top role | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Ukraine's military intelligence chief, Kyrylo Budanov, has been appointed as President Volodymyr Zelenksyy's new chief of staff. The new role places Budanov at the center of decision-making in the war against Russia - and in US-led peace negotiations. The shake-up comes after his predecessor resigned over a corruption scandal, that left voters furious. 
00:00 Ukrainian President Zelenskyy appoints Kyrylo Budanov as chief of staff.
Mikhail Alexseev, Professor of Political Science at the San Diego State University speaks to DW

#zelenskyy #ukrainewar

items.title: Why Trump is really threatening Iran’s leadership over protest crackdown | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>There are rising tensions between Washington and Tehran. The US president has threatened to intervene against the crackdown on growing anti-government demonstrations across Iran. 
In a misspelt social media post, President Trump warned: "If Iran shoots and violently kills peaceful protesters, which is their custom, the United States of America will come to their rescue. We are locked and loaded and ready to go".  

00:00 US and Iranian leaders exchange threats
02:31 Alex Vatanka an Iran expert and senior fellow at the Middle East Institute in Washington D.C. talks to DW
06:39 DW talks to Kamran Matin Associate Professor of International Relations, at the University of Sussex in the United Kingdom.

#iran #trump
